Semantic Rule Case ID Feature Overall Coverage 详细信息
SR-MR-001-01Coverage: partially_covered | Req positivenegative|Present negativepositive|Accept positive|Missing negative
SR-MR-001-01TC-SR-MR-001-01-positive-01LME Rulebook Defined Termsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-001-01-positive-01
Title: Reference to LME Rulebook for undefined terms
Intent: Verify that undefined capitalised terms are interpreted according to the LME Rulebook
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-001-01
Given
  • A term is used in matching rules documentation
  • The term is capitalised but not explicitly defined in the matching rules
When
  • The term's meaning must be determined
Then
  • The term shall have the meaning ascribed to it in the LME Rulebook
Assumptions
  • The LME Rulebook is accessible and contains the relevant definitions
Evidence
MR-001-01: Capitalised terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the meaning ascribed to them in the Rules and Regulations of the LME (p.2)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings 
SR-MR-001-01TC-SR-MR-001-01-negative-01LME Rulebook Defined Termsmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-001-01-negative-01
Title: Term not in LME Rulebook causes ambiguity
Intent: Verify that undefined terms without LME Rulebook reference result in ambiguity
Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-001-01
Given
  • A capitalised term is used in matching rules documentation
  • The term is not defined in the matching rules
  • The term is not found in the LME Rulebook
When
  • The term's meaning must be determined
Then
  • The term's meaning cannot be reliably established
  • Compliance interpretation may be required
Assumptions
  • A mechanism exists for reporting undefined terms to the Exchange
Evidence
MR-001-01: Capitalised terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the meaning ascribed to them in the Rules and Regulations of the LME (p.2)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Maker evidence quote is incomplete - the full quote from MR-001-01 includes the parenthetical about the LME Rulebook, but the scenario evidence omits this
The 'assumptions' about a mechanism for reporting undefined terms is not supported by explicit rule evidence - this is an inference without source backing
The negative case type is acceptable but tests an inferred boundary condition rather than explicit non-compliance: the rule defines how terms SHOULD be interpreted (positive obligation) but does not define consequences for missing terms
SR-MR-001-02Coverage: fully_covered | Req positive|Present positive|Accept positive
SR-MR-001-02TC-SR-MR-001-02-positive-01LME Matching Rules Administrative Proceduresmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-001-02-positive-01
Title: Matching rules adopted as Administrative Procedures
Intent: Verify that matching rules are part of Administrative Procedures adopted by the Exchange
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-001-02
Given
  • The LME matching rules document exists
  • The Exchange (LME) operates under an LME Rulebook
When
  • The matching rules are applied to business transactions
Then
  • The matching rules form part of Administrative Procedures
  • The matching rules are adopted in accordance with the LME Rulebook
Assumptions
  • The Administrative Procedures are current and valid
Evidence
MR-001-02: These matching rules form part of the Administrative Procedures that may be adopted by the Exchange (p.2)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote minor punctuation variant - quote has 'the Exchange' vs semantic rule 'the Exchange(also referred to as the "LME")' - functionally equivalent but not verbatim
The 'when' clause lacks explicit verification steps - tests that adoption SHOULD happen rather than HOW to verify it occurred

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-003-01-positive-01
Title: Member contacts Exchange after price validation failure
Intent: Verify that Members are required to contact the Exchange when an order or trade fails price validation
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-003-01
Given
  • A Member submits an order or trade
  • The order or trade fails the price validation check
When
  • The price validation check returns a failure result
Then
  • The Member is required to contact the Exchange
  • The Member must explain the rationale for the price of the rejected order or trade
Assumptions
  • The Member has access to contact Exchange operations
Evidence
MR-003-01: Where an order or trade fails the price validation check, Members are required to contact the Exchange (p.2)

Title: Member fails to contact Exchange after price validation failure
Intent: Verify non-compliance when Member does not contact Exchange after price validation failure
Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-003-01
Given
  • A Member submits an order or trade
  • The order or trade fails the price validation check
  • The Member is aware of the failure
When
  • The Member does not contact the Exchange to explain the price rationale
Then
  • The Member is non-compliant with matching rules
  • The rejected order or trade remains unresolved
Assumptions
  • The system logs the absence of Exchange contact for the failed validation

Intent: Verify that Members may provide additional information for correction of failed checks and re-submission

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-003-02
Given
  • A Member has failed checks on an order or trade
  • The Member wishes to correct the failed checks
When
  • The Member provides additional information as required by the Exchange
Then
  • The Exchange evaluates whether the order or trade should be permitted to be re-submitted in its original form

SR-MR-003-03TC-SR-MR-003-03-positive-01Collateral Requirement for Trade Re-submissionmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-003-03-positive-01
Title: Member posts additional collateral after re-submission permission
Intent: Verify that Members must comply with additional collateral requirements when re-submission is permitted
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-003-03
Given
  • A Member has a failed order or trade
  • The Exchange permits re-submission of the order or trade
When
  • The Exchange determines additional collateral is required
Then
  • The Member must post additional collateral to the Clearing House
  • The Member must comply with such collateral requirements
Assumptions
  • The Member has sufficient credit facilities to post additional collateral
Evidence
MR-003-03: there may be a requirement to post additional collateral to the Clearing House, and Members must comply with such requirements (p.2)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Semantic rule execution_mapping.action is 'use_code' - mismatch with scenario which requires 'post_collateral' action. The rule language says 'must comply' (obligation) but semantic rule classifies as 'permission' with 'may' mode
Semantic rule object is 'unspecified_object' but scenario context clearly implies 'collateral' - semantic rule under-specified
Evidence quote correctly cited but semantic rule construction has integrity issues that reduce alignment confidence

When

  • The request is evaluated based on the nature of the stated exceptional circumstances
Assumptions
  • The Exchange defines what constitutes exceptional circumstances

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-008-01-positive-01
Title: Member retains full audit trail for all covered activities
Intent: Verify that members who retain a complete audit trail for orders, non-electronic agreed trades, and post-trade operations comply with the retention obligation
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-01
Given
  • A member has submitted orders to LME Select
  • A member has agreed trades non-electronically
  • A member has performed post-trade operations
When
  • The member retains audit trail records for all these activities
Then
  • The processing result is not null
  • The retention obligation is fulfilled
Assumptions
  • Audit trail contains all required particulars
  • Records are retained for required duration
Evidence
MR-008-01: Members must retain a full audit trail for all orders submitted to LME Select (p.4)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote weakness: scenario evidence 'Members must retain a full audit trail for all orders submitted to LME Select' captures only the first component of a three-part obligation. The full semantic rule encompasses 'all Agreed Trades agreed non-electronically, and all post-trade operations' in addition to orders. The test scenario correctly covers all three components in the given section, but the evidence citation is incomplete.

Title: Member fails to retain audit trail for covered activities

Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-01

  • A member has submitted orders to LME Select
  • A member has agreed trades non-electronically
  • A member has performed post-trade operations
When
  • The member does not retain audit trail records for these activities
Then
  • The retention obligation is not fulfilled
  • Compliance validation fails
Assumptions
  • Missing audit trail is detectable through compliance review

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-008-02-positive-01
Title: Audit trail includes USD price and FX rate for non-USD transactions
Intent: Verify that when a member executes a transaction in a non-US Dollar currency, the audit trail correctly includes the relevant US Dollars price and the FX rate used
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-02
Given
  • A member has executed a transaction in Euro
  • The transaction involves a non-US Dollar currency
When
  • The member creates or updates the audit trail for the transaction
Then
  • The audit trail includes the relevant US Dollars price
  • The audit trail includes the FX rate used to calculate the non-US Dollars price
Assumptions
  • The FX rate used is a valid market rate at the time of execution
Evidence
MR-008-02: Where Members have Executed transactions in currencies other than US Dollars, the audit trail for these transactions must include the relevant US Dollars price and the FX rate used to calculate the non-US Dollars price. (p.4)

Case Type Accepted: True

SR-MR-010-01TC-SR-MR-010-01-positive-01Carry Trade Entry Requirementsmissingcovered

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-010-01-positive-01
Title: Carry trades entered correctly with clear audit trail designation
Intent: Verify that agreed Carry trades are entered as such in the Matching System with proper audit trail indicating trade type
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-01
Given
  • Two members have agreed on a trade that is a Carry
When
  • The members enter the trade into the Matching System as a Carry
Then
  • The trade is accepted in the Matching System
  • The audit trail clearly demonstrates the trade is a Carry
  • The audit trail distinguishes the trade as Carry rather than outright
Assumptions
  • All other trade parameters are valid
Evidence
MR-010-01: Agreed trades that are agreed as Carries should be entered as such in to the Matching System and the audit trail for the transaction should clearly demonstrate whether a trade is an outright or a Carry. (p.4)

SR-MR-010-01TC-SR-MR-010-01-negative-01Carry Trade Entry Requirementsmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-010-01-negative-01
Title: Carry trades entered as outright trades without clear designation
Intent: Verify that the system prevents or flags Carry trades that are not properly designated in the Matching System
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-01
Given
  • Two members have agreed on a trade that is a Carry
When
  • The members enter the trade into the Matching System without proper Carry designation
  • The members enter the trade as an outright trade
Then
  • The system flags the incorrect entry
  • The members receive a warning or rejection
Assumptions
  • The system has capability to validate trade type designation
Evidence
MR-010-01: should be entered as such in to the Matching System (p.4)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence weakness: The quote 'should be entered as such in to the Matching System' describes a behavioral expectation but does not explicitly establish a validation/enforcement mechanism in the system.
The scenario assumption 'The system has capability to validate trade type designation' is an inference not directly supported by the rule text.
The evidence does not confirm that the system will reject, warn, or flag incorrect Carry designations—it only states what should happen, not what the system does when it doesn't.
Negative test assumes active enforcement where the rule may only describe a convention without guaranteed system-level validation.
SR-MR-010-02Coverage: fully_covered | Req positivenegative|Present negativepositive|Accept negativepositive

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-010-02-positive-01
Title: Carry trades submitted with legs in correct chronological order
Intent: Verify that when submitting Carry trades, members enter the near leg first followed by other legs in chronological prompt date order
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-02
Given
  • A member is submitting a Carry trade with multiple legs
  • The near leg has the earliest prompt date
When
  • The member enters the near leg first
  • The member enters subsequent legs in chronological prompt date order
Then
  • The Carry trade is accepted in the Matching System
  • The leg order follows market convention
Assumptions
  • All prompt dates are valid and in chronological order
Evidence
MR-010-02: Members must follow market convention and enter the near leg first followed by all other legs in chronological prompt date order. (p.4)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

Title: Carry trades submitted with legs in incorrect order

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-02
Given
  • A member is submitting a Carry trade with multiple legs
When
  • The member enters a later leg before the near leg
  • The member enters legs in non-chronological order
Then
  • The system rejects the submission or flags the incorrect order
  • The member receives an error indicating leg order violation
Assumptions
  • The system validates leg order against market convention

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence subtlety: The quote 'enter the near leg first followed by all other legs in chronological prompt date order' describes the correct behavior but does not explicitly state rejection behavior for incorrect order.
The scenario assumes system rejection/flagging when the rule only mandates the convention without explicitly stating enforcement.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-011-01-positive-01
Title: Subsequent legs entered as outright trades after near leg expiration
Intent: Verify that when adjusting a Member-to-Member Carry trade after near leg expiration, subsequent legs are entered as outright trades
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-011-01
Given
  • A Member-to-Member Carry trade exists with the near leg now expired
  • There is a requirement to reverse or correct the trade
When
  • The member enters subsequent legs to the Matching System as outright trades
Then
  • The subsequent legs are accepted as outright trades
  • The adjustment is recorded correctly in the system
Assumptions
  • The reversal or correction is a valid business requirement
Evidence

Title: Subsequent legs incorrectly entered as Carry trades after adjustment
Intent: Verify that the system prevents or flags incorrect entry of subsequent legs as Carry trades when they should be outright
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-011-01
Given
  • A Member-to-Member Carry trade requires adjustment after near leg expiration
When
  • The member attempts to enter subsequent legs as Carry trades instead of outright trades
Then
  • The system flags or rejects the incorrect entry
  • The member is notified to enter the legs as outright trades
Assumptions
  • The system has capability to distinguish between Carry and outright trade types during adjustment

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence subtlety: The quote 'should be entered to the Matching System as outright trades' describes the required behavior but does not explicitly state rejection behavior for incorrect Carry entry.
The scenario assumes the system can distinguish and reject Carry trade entries during adjustment—this is a reasonable inference but not explicitly confirmed in the rule.

SR-MR-012-01TC-SR-MR-012-01-negative-01Prohibition of Abusive Practices on LME Base Servicemissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-012-01-negative-01
Title: Member attempt to use LME Base Service for abusive practice rejected
Intent: Verify that the system rejects a member's attempt to use the LME Base Service for an abusive practice such as avoiding or evading the Rules
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-012-01
Given
  • A member is authenticated and authorized to access the LME Base Service
  • The member has intent to conduct a transaction that constitutes an abusive practice
When
  • The member attempts to include_field with an abusive intent pattern
Then
  • The system returns validation_result equal to reject
  • The system prevents the abusive action from being processed
Assumptions
  • Abusive practice detection is active
  • The system has access to transaction context for abuse pattern matching
Evidence
MR-012-01: Members must not use the LME Base Service for any abusive practice (p.5)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Scenario correctly tests the prohibition: system must reject abusive practices.
Evidence alignment: scenario evidence quote matches semantic rule statement.
Well-structured Gherkin steps: GIVEN sets up authenticated member with abusive intent, WHEN attempts the prohibited action, THEN expects rejection.
Assumptions about abuse detection being active are reasonable prerequisites.

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-012-01
Given
  • A member is authenticated and authorized to access the LME Base Service
  • The member intends to conduct legitimate, compliant business
When
  • The member includes_field for a legitimate trading purpose
Then
  • The system processes the request without rejection
  • The processing_result indicates successful submission

Priority: high

Given
  • A member has valid trade data for a transaction they are party to
When
  • The member enters their own order/trade data to the Matching System

  • The processing_result is not null indicating successful processing

SR-MR-013-01TC-SR-MR-013-01-negative-01Member Obligation to Enter Own Trade Datamissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-013-01-negative-01
Title: Member attempt to rely on other party's trade data rejected
Intent: Verify that the system prevents or flags a member from relying on trade data entered by the other party
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-013-01
Given
  • A member is party to a trade
  • The other party has already entered their order/trade data
When
  • The member attempts to enter_trade_data relying on the other party's submitted data
Then
  • The system validates the trade entry and identifies the data source mismatch
  • The system flags or rejects the entry requiring the member to enter their own data
Assumptions
  • The system can differentiate between data entered by different parties
  • Validation rules can detect data source inconsistencies
Evidence
MR-013-01: they must use their own order/trade data and not rely upon that entered by the other party (p.5)

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Scenario correctly targets the negative obligation: 'must not rely upon that entered by the other party'.
Evidence quote directly supports the scenario intent.
Test design appropriately sets up context where other party has already entered data, then tests for data source mismatch detection.
Assumption that system can differentiate between data entered by different parties is reasonable but depends on implementation capability.

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-014-B2-1

When

Then

  • The processing_result is not null indicating successful categorization

SR-MR-014-B2-1TC-SR-MR-014-B2-1-negative-01Normal Trade Category Definitionmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-014-B2-1-negative-01
Title: Trade incorrectly assigned Normal category when not qualifying
Intent: Verify that trades that do not meet Normal category criteria are not incorrectly assigned that category
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-014-B2-1
Given
  • A registered_intermediating_broker is entering trade data
  • The trade does not meet Normal category criteria (not Ring, Basis Ring, inter-office, or Matching System)
When
  • The broker attempts to assign the Normal category to the trade
Then
  • The system validates the venue_code and trade origin
  • The system rejects the Normal categorization or requires alternative enum_value
Assumptions
  • The system validates trade category against venue and execution method
  • Alternative valid categories exist for non-qualifying trades
Evidence
MR-014-B2-1: Normal – is used to enter new market trades Executed on the Ring, Basis Ring (p.5)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
WEAKENED EVIDENCE: The semantic rule MR-014-B2-1 is an enum DEFINITION (informational), not an enforceable rule with validation logic.
The negative test assumes the system validates trade category against venue and execution method, but this enforcement is NOT stated in the evidence.
Evidence quote only defines what Normal category IS; it does not state that the system rejects incorrect categorization.
Higher hallucination risk: test design implies validation capability that may not exist in the system under test.
Recommendation: Clarify whether system enforces category validation before executing negative test.

Title: Transfer category used for moving positions between Members on same business day

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-014-B9-1
Given
  • A member has a registered single trade or group of trades
  • The member intends to move the position to another Member
When
  • The member registers the trade using the 'Transfer' category
  • The submission occurs within the business day cutoff
Then
  • The Transfer registration is accepted
  • The position move is recorded successfully
Assumptions
  • The deadline is the business day cutoff with 0 business day offset

The semantic rule SR-MR-014-B9-1 is classified as a 'deadline' rule with business_day_cutoff, but the scenario evidence does not cite this timing constraint directly.
The scenario assumptions correctly introduce the deadline aspect (business day cutoff with 0 offset), but this information is not sourced from the provided evidence quote.
Risk of hallucination: deadline timing requirement appears to be inferred rather than directly evidenced.

SR-MR-015-B2-1TC-SR-MR-015-B2-1-positive-01Current Price Type Obligation for Market Price Tradesmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-015-B2-1-positive-01
Title: Current price type correctly applied for trades at current market prices
Intent: Verify that the 'Current' price type is used for Agreed Trades at current market prices with entry on TD+0 or TD+1
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B2-1
Given
  • A trade is arranged at current market prices
  • The trade will be entered to the Matching System on TD+0 or TD+1
When
  • The member matches the trade using the 'Current' price type
Then
  • The trade matching is accepted
  • The Current price type is recorded for the transaction
Assumptions
  • The trade entry occurs on TD+0 or TD+1 as specified
Evidence
MR-015-B2-1: Current – must be used to record Agreed Trades arranged at current market prices (p.6)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Strong evidence: The quote directly states 'must be used to record Agreed Trades arranged at current market prices'.
The scenario correctly tests obligation fulfillment when Current price type is applied.
TD+0 or TD+1 timing condition in assumptions aligns with the full evidence quote.
SR-MR-015-B2-1TC-SR-MR-015-B2-1-negative-01Current Price Type Obligation for Market Price Tradesmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-015-B2-1-negative-01
Title: Trade at current market prices rejected when using non-Current price type
Intent: Verify that trades at current market prices cannot use a price type other than 'Current'
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B2-1
Given
  • A trade is arranged at current market prices
  • The trade will be entered to the Matching System on TD+0 or TD+1
When
  • The member attempts to match the trade using a price type other than 'Current'
Then
  • The trade matching is rejected
  • The system indicates the Current price type is required
Assumptions
  • The system enforces the price type obligation for market price trades
Evidence
MR-015-B2-1: Current – must be used to record Agreed Trades arranged at current market prices (p.6)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Strong evidence supports the negative test scenario.
The scenario correctly tests that using a non-Current price type for market price trades violates the obligation.
Well-designed complementary negative case.

Title: Historic price type used for non-electronic transactions
Intent: Verify that Historic price type is correctly used to record transactions that are not executed electronically
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B3-1
Given
  • A member records a transaction that is not electronically executed
When
  • The member assigns the 'Historic' price type to the transaction
Then
  • The transaction is accepted and recorded successfully
  • The price type field is set to 'Historic'
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-015-B3-1: Historic – must be used to record all other transactions(e.g. (p.6)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
WEAK EVIDENCE: The evidence quote is truncated: 'Historic – must be used to record all other transactions(e.g.' - the example context is missing.
The complete rule likely specifies what 'all other transactions' means (e.g., non-electronic transactions), but this is not evidenced.
Risk of hallucination: The scenario interprets 'all other transactions' as 'not electronically executed', but this clarification is not in the provided evidence.
The evidence truncation may lead to incomplete understanding of the rule's scope.
SR-MR-015-B3-1TC-SR-MR-015-B3-1-negative-01Historic Price Type Requirementmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-015-B3-1-negative-01
Title: Historic price type rejected for electronic transactions
Intent: Verify that Historic price type is not accepted for electronically executed business
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B3-1
Given
  • A transaction is executed electronically via LME Select
When
  • The member attempts to assign the 'Historic' price type to the transaction
Then
  • The transaction is rejected or the price type override is not permitted
  • The system requires the 'Current' price type for electronic execution
Assumptions
  • Electronic execution via LME Select mandates 'Current' price type per MR-015-B3-2
Evidence
MR-015-B3-1: Historic – must be used to record all other transactions(e.g. (p.6)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct

  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}

Missing Aspects

Evidence quote is incomplete (cuts off at 'e.g.') - weakens citation strength
Assumption references MR-015-B3-2 which is outside scope of MR-015-B3-1 - cross-rule inference should be noted
Rule statement implies Historic is for 'all other transactions' which aligns with the negative test that Historic should not be used for electronic execution

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-015-B3-3-positive-01
Title: Trade submitted within 10-minute deadline is accepted
Intent: Verify that trades submitted within 10 minutes of agreement are accepted for matching
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B3-3
Given
  • An Agreed Trade is formed at time T
  • The member has a valid trade ready for matching
When
  • The trade is submitted to the matching system at time T+9 minutes
Then
  • The trade is accepted for matching
  • The deadline validation result is 'pass'
  • The trade timestamp is recorded as T+9 minutes
Assumptions
  • Submission occurs within the same business day
Evidence
MR-015-B3-3: Because of the requirement to match trades within 10 minutes of Agreement of an Agreed Trade (p.6)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores

  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote is complete and directly supports the positive scenario
Test design is well-structured with clear Given/When/Then
T+9 minutes is a valid interior point within the 10-minute deadline

Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B3-3
Given
  • An Agreed Trade is formed at time T
  • The member has a valid trade ready for matching
When
  • The trade is submitted to the matching system at exactly T+10 minutes
Then
  • The trade is accepted at the boundary
  • The deadline validation result is 'pass'

  • System clock precision allows for exact boundary submissions
  • Interpretation of 'within 10 minutes' includes the endpoint

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-015-B3-4
Given

  • The transaction is within the member's own systems
When
  • The member creates the transaction with complete audit trail documentation
Then
  • The average price transaction is permitted
  • The audit trail is recorded and maintained
  • The transaction is associated with a single Client
Assumptions
  • The audit trail includes all required action documentation per paragraph 33

Title: Valid Settlement Price code calculation for outright trade
Intent: Verify that Settlement Price (S) code correctly calculates price based on the day's Settlement Price

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-017-B2-1
Given
  • A trade is being submitted for matching
  • The price for the outright trade is based on the day's Settlement Price
When
  • The short_price_code is set to 'S' with price_inputs containing the Settlement Price
Then
  • The calculated_value for the trade is based on the Settlement Price
  • The processing_result is not null
Assumptions
  • The Settlement Price for the relevant Prompt Date is available

SR-MR-017-B2-1TC-SR-MR-017-B2-1-boundary-01Price Code Calculation for Settlement and Closing Pricesmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-017-B2-1-boundary-01
Title: Closing Price code at end of trading day boundary
Intent: Verify Closing Price code calculation handles the end of trading day boundary correctly
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-017-B2-1
Given
  • A Carry trade is being submitted near end of trading day
  • The Closing Price for the relevant Prompt Date is being finalized
When
  • The short_price_code is set to 'C' for each Carry leg at the closing boundary
Then
  • The Closing Price code calculates each leg price based on the day's Closing Price
  • The calculated_value includes any agreed premium or discount
Assumptions
  • Closing Price is published at the end of the trading session
Evidence
MR-017-B2-1: Closing Price("C") Code – where the price is based upon that day's Closing Price for the relevant Prompt Date for each outright trade or Carry leg (p.7)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
The boundary test for end-of-trading-day scenario is appropriate for testing the temporal boundary condition of when Closing Price becomes available.
Evidence quote correctly references the atomic rule MR-017-B2-1 on page 7.
However, the test does not explicitly verify the inter-office venue requirement mentioned in the rule. Consider adding venue verification to strengthen test completeness.

Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-017-B4-1
Given
  • A Carry trade is being submitted
  • The parties have agreed to no premium or discount (zero adjustment)
When
  • The short_price_code is set to 'B' with zero premium or discount
Then
  • The other leg(s) pricing is calculated relative to the first leg with zero adjustment
  • The calculated_value accurately reflects the zero adjustment

This could more appropriately be classified as a positive test with edge case data, rather than a boundary test.
Consider reclassifying as a positive test or defining a clearer boundary condition.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-018-01-boundary-01
Title: Substitution handles price differential across carry legs
Intent: Verify boundary case where carry has multiple legs with different price differentials
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-018-01
Given
  • A Carry trade has multiple legs with short price codes
  • Each leg references the near leg plus or minus premium/discount
When
  • The Matching System calculates absolute values for all legs
Then
  • Absolute values are correctly calculated for each leg containing a price differential
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-018-01: calculate absolute values for all leg(s) containing a price differential (p.8)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence supports multi-leg differential calculation: 'calculate absolute values for all leg(s) containing a price differential' (MR-018-01, page 8)
Test appropriately exercises the boundary condition of multiple carry legs with price differentials
Design quality is strong - tests the calculation complexity across multiple legs
SR-MR-018-01TC-SR-MR-018-01-data_validation-01Short Price Code Substitutionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-018-01-data_validation-01
Title: System handles missing absolute value data
Intent: Verify that the system validates required data availability before substitution
Priority: high
Case Type: data_validation
Paragraph IDs: MR-018-01
Given
  • A trade has been submitted with a short price code
  • The relevant absolute value has NOT been made available
When
  • The Matching System attempts to process the trade
Then
  • The system either defers substitution or flags pending status
  • Substitution does not proceed with null values
Assumptions
  • Data availability is a prerequisite for substitution
Evidence
MR-018-01: once the relevant absolute value has been made available (p.8)
Case Type: data_validation
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence supports prerequisite validation: 'once the relevant absolute value has been made available' (MR-018-01, page 8)
Test correctly validates that substitution does not proceed when data is unavailable
Appropriate data validation scenario for the timing dependency in the rule

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-019-01-positive-01
Title: Trade matched within deadline passes validation
Intent: Verify that trades submitted within the matching deadline are accepted
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-019-01
Given
  • A contract is submitted for matching
  • Submission occurs within the deadline defined in Regulation 3.5
When
  • The Matching System validates the submission timing
Then
  • The deadline validation result is pass
  • The trade is accepted for matching
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-019-01: Regulation 3.5 of Part 3, of the Rules sets out the deadlines for matching Contracts (p.8)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is weak: quote 'Regulation 3.5 of Part 3, of the Rules sets out the deadlines' only references another document without providing specific deadline values
The actual deadline time/parameters are not captured in the rule evidence - test cannot be definitively validated without consulting external Regulation 3.5
Evidence consistency reduced due to indirect reference; the rule itself lacks concrete timing parameters
Requirement coverage is partial because specific deadline values are not defined in the semantic rule

When

Then

  • An Agreed Trade is agreed in Ring or inter-office market
  • The trade uses Official, Mean, or Closing Price as basis
  • The Exchange has made all relevant data available (prices declared final after objection period)
When
  • Trade data is entered with the relevant Ring time code
  • Submission occurs within 10 minutes of data availability
Then
  • The deadline validation result is pass
  • The trade is accepted for matching

  • A RIB has registered an Agreed Trade in the Matching System

Then
  • The Matching System records the approval with a pass deadline validation result

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-022-01-boundary-01
Title: Clearing Member approves at exact deadline boundary
Intent: Verify system behavior when approval occurs exactly at the 10-minute deadline boundary
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-022-01
Given
  • A RIB has registered an Agreed Trade in the Matching System
  • Exactly 9 minutes and 59 seconds have elapsed
When
  • The Clearing Member submits approval at the 10-minute boundary
Then
  • The Matching System accepts the approval as within deadline
Assumptions
  • System clock precision is sufficient to distinguish submissions at the boundary
Evidence
MR-022-01: within 10 minutes of such registration (p.9)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Well-constructed boundary scenario testing exactly at 9:59 elapsed seconds
Reasonable assumption about system clock precision is documented
Minor note: The rule does not explicitly clarify whether exactly 10 minutes is inclusive or exclusive, but the maker's interpretation (inclusive boundary at 9:59.999) is pragmatically sound

SR-MR-022-02TC-SR-MR-022-02-positive-01PTT Order Arrangement Timemissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-022-02-positive-01
Title: PTT Order arrangement time correctly identified
Intent: Verify that for PTT Orders, the arrangement time is correctly determined from Pre-Trade Communications between two Clients
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-022-02
Given
  • An Agreed Trade comprises a PTT Order
  • Pre-Trade Communications exist between two Clients that would form an IOM PTT Initiating Pair
When
  • The system determines the time of arrangement for the PTT Order
Then
  • The arrangement time is set to the time from which the RIB has the Pre-Trade Communications
Assumptions
  • Pre-Trade Communications are timestamped and accessible to the RIB
Evidence
MR-022-02: the time of arrangement shall be the time from which the RIB has Pre-Trade Communications in respect of two Clients (p.9)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is consistent with the semantic rule
The semantic rule itself has inference flags: actor and action are unspecified_inferred, which may reduce test precision
Maker's assumption about timestamped Pre-Trade Communications is reasonable and appropriate
SR-MR-022-02TC-SR-MR-022-02-negative-01PTT Order Arrangement Timemissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-022-02-negative-01
Title: PTT Order with no qualifying Pre-Trade Communications
Intent: Verify system behavior when determining arrangement time for a PTT Order without valid Pre-Trade Communications
Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-022-02
Given
  • An Agreed Trade is submitted as a PTT Order
  • No qualifying Pre-Trade Communications exist between two Clients
When
  • The system attempts to determine the arrangement time
Then
  • The system cannot establish a valid arrangement time and returns an error or requires manual intervention
Assumptions
  • The matching system requires traceable Pre-Trade Communications for PTT arrangement time
Evidence
MR-022-02: the time from which the RIB has Pre-Trade Communications in respect of two Clients that would comprise an IOM PTT Initiating Pair (p.9)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True

Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is consistent but draws an inference (system requires Pre-Trade Communications) that is implied but not explicitly stated in the rule
The rule does not specify what happens when Pre-Trade Communications are absent; the maker infers an error or manual intervention outcome
This inference is reasonable but should be validated against actual system requirements

Title: Ring venue trade time with invalid code rejected
Intent: Verify that Ring venue trades reject invalid Trade Time codes
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-023-B3-1
Given
  • A trade is being submitted with venue code indicating Ring
When
  • The Trade Time field is entered with an invalid code not equal to R1 or K1
Then
  • The system rejects the Trade Time value and returns a validation error
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-023-B3-1: Ring – either R 1 if Executed during a Ring session or K 1 if Executed during a kerb session (p.9)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote shows 'R 1' (with space) but scenario expects 'R1' or 'K1' (no space). Alignment between evidence and expected behavior should be clarified—assumed system normalizes whitespace which is reasonable but not stated in rule.
SR-MR-023-B3-1TC-SR-MR-023-B3-1-data_validation-01Ring Venue Trade Time Formatmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-023-B3-1-data_validation-01
Title: Ring venue trade time code format validation
Intent: Verify that the Trade Time code for Ring venue matches the expected format and allowed values
Priority: high
Case Type: data_validation
Paragraph IDs: MR-023-B3-1
Given
  • A trade is being submitted with venue code indicating Ring
When
  • The Trade Time field is entered with variations in casing, spacing, or extra characters
Then
  • The system validates the format and accepts R1, r1 variations while rejecting malformed inputs
Assumptions
  • System may normalize casing and whitespace for the code validation
Evidence
MR-023-B3-1: Ring – either R 1 if Executed during a Ring session or K 1 if Executed during a kerb session (p.9)
Case Type: data_validation
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence weakness: Rule evidence only specifies 'R 1' and 'K 1' as valid codes. The scenario's assumption that system normalizes casing/whitespace ('r1', 'R1' variations accepted) is not supported by explicit evidence.
Evidence quote uses 'R 1' with space, but scenario expects R1 variations without space—unresolved ambiguity.
The 'then' clause states system 'accepts R1, r1 variations' but this specific behavior is not derived from rule evidence.

Case Type: positive

  • The trade is agreed during a permissible Ring or relevant kerb session

Then

SR-MR-024-D-1TC-SR-MR-024-D-1-positive-01Matching System Account Typesmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-D-1-positive-01
Title: Trade matched with valid account type code
Intent: Verify that the Matching System accepts trades with valid account type codes H, U, C, G, or S
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-D-1
Given
  • A trade is being submitted to the Matching System
  • The appropriate account type is identified for the business type
When
  • The account field is set to a valid code: H for House, U for Unallocated, C for Client OSA, G for Client GOSA, or S for Client ISA
Then
  • The Matching System records the trade with the specified account type and returns a successful processing result
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-024-D-1: The Matching System supports the following accounts: - H – House, for all proprietary, non-Client business (p.9)
MR-024-D-1: U – Unallocated, sub-account of H, for all business that is to be given-up to another Clearing Member (p.9)
MR-024-D-1: C – Client, for all business allocated to a net omnibus segregated Client account (p.9)
MR-024-D-1: G – Client, for all business allocated to a gross omnibus segregated Client account (p.9)
MR-024-D-1: S – Client, for all business allocated to an individual segregated Client account (p.9)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-D-1-negative-01
Title: Trade matched with invalid account type code rejected
Intent: Verify that the Matching System rejects trades with invalid account type codes
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-D-1
Given
  • A trade is being submitted to the Matching System
When
  • The account field is set to an invalid code not in the allowed set of H, U, C, G, or S
Then
  • The Matching System rejects the submission and returns a validation error
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-024-D-1: The Matching System supports the following accounts (p.9)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is weak: The quote 'The Matching System supports the following accounts' defines valid values but does not explicitly state that invalid values are rejected or result in a validation error.
The negative outcome (rejection/validation error) is an inference from the enum definition rather than an explicit rule statement.
Recommendation: The maker should have cited additional supporting evidence that explicitly addresses error handling for invalid enum values, or clarified that rejection behavior is implied by the specification.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-E-1-positive-01
Title: Agreed Trade Executed When Matched and Pre-Execution Checks Satisfied
Intent: Verify that an Agreed Trade satisfying Acceptance Criteria is executed after Matching System confirmation and Pre-Execution Checks completion
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-E-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade exists that satisfies Acceptance Criteria
  • A Fixed Price Auction (if applicable) has completed
When
  • The Matching System confirms the Agreed Trade is matched
  • All Pre-Execution Checks are satisfied
Then
  • The Agreed Trade shall be Executed
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-024-E-1: the Agreed Trade shall, provided that it satisfies the Acceptance Criteria, be Executed at the time, following the completion of any Fixed Price Auction, that the Matching System confirms that the Agreed Trade is matched (p.9)

Case Type Accepted: True

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-E-1-negative-01
Title: Trade Not Executed When Pre-Execution Checks Fail
Intent: Verify that execution does not occur when Pre-Execution Checks are not satisfied
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-E-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade exists that satisfies Acceptance Criteria
  • The Matching System has confirmed the trade is matched
When
  • One or more Pre-Execution Checks are not satisfied
Then
  • The Agreed Trade shall not be Executed
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-024-E-1: that all Pre-Execution Checks are satisfied (p.9)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is weak: The quote 'that all Pre-Execution Checks are satisfied' states the condition but does not explicitly state the consequence when the condition is NOT satisfied.
The maker inferred that execution shall not occur when Pre-Execution Checks fail, but this negation is not directly stated in the source evidence.
The scenario's negative outcome ('shall not be Executed') is a logical negation of the rule's positive obligation rather than an explicit rule statement.

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-026-01

When
  • The Member identifies an incorrect account where the correct account should be identified
Then

Missing Aspects

The scenario outcome states 'trade may be allocated to an X Account', but the semantic rule does not explicitly mandate X Account as the only consequence. The language 'may be' indicates uncertainty in the specification. However, this does not invalidate the test case—it appropriately exercises the negative path of the obligation.

SR-MR-030-01TC-SR-MR-030-01-positive-01Physical Metal Swap 4-Way Agreement Requirementmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-030-01-positive-01
Title: Member enters physical metal swap transaction with 4-way agreement in place
Intent: Verify that members can enter trade data for non-common client physical metal swaps when required 4-way agreement is established
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-030-01
Given
  • Member is registered and authorized on the LME Matching System
  • Member has a valid 4-way agreement in place for non-common client transactions
When
  • Member enters trade data for a physical metal swap for a non-common Client
Then
  • Trade data is accepted by the system
  • Transaction proceeds for processing
Assumptions
  • The 4-way agreement remains valid and has not expired
  • Non-common client status has been verified
Evidence
MR-030-01: Members are required to have a 4-way agreement in place prior to entering such a transaction (p.11)

  • Member does not have a 4-way agreement in place for non-common client transactions

Then
  • Trade data entry is rejected by the system
  • Error message indicates missing 4-way agreement requirement

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-030-03-positive-01
Title: Member confirms all client requirements for metal swap transaction
Intent: Verify that member can confirm all required client conditions via the agreement before proceeding with metal swap
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-030-03
Given
  • Member has a client requesting a metal swap transaction
  • Client has provided written instructions for the transaction
When
  • Member confirms via the agreement that: client is a direct customer, client is a commercial user of the metal concerned, and client has given written instructions
Then
  • Confirmation is recorded
  • Transaction proceeds with all client requirements verified
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-030-03: Each Member must confirm via the agreement that the Client is a direct customer, is a commercial user of the metal concerned and had given the Member written instructions regarding the metal swap transaction (p.11)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-032-01-positive-01
Title: Clearing member transfers position using Transfer post-trade category

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-032-01
Given
  • Clearing Member A holds a Client position in a GOSA/OSA/ISA account for Base Service
  • Client position is being transferred to Clearing Member B
When
  • Clearing Member A moves the position out of the relevant GOSA/OSA/ISA account to the Member's House account using the Transfer post-trade category
Then
  • Position is successfully moved to House account
  • Transfer post-trade category is applied correctly
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-032-01: The position must first be moved out of the relevant GOSA/OSA/ISA account to the Member's House account using the Transfer post-trade category (p.11)

Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-033-01
Given
  • Clearing member is transferring a client position between accounts
When
  • Clearing member specifies transfer price as either the original Historic contract price or Current market price (Closing Price for relevant Prompt Date)
Then
  • Transfer is processed at the specified price
  • Price selection is recorded with the transfer
Assumptions
  • Current market price refers to Closing Price for the relevant Prompt Date

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-033-02-positive-01
Title: Valid weighted average calculation with audit trail
Intent: Verify member can transfer weighted average prices when sufficient audit trail exists
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-033-02
Given
  • A member has multiple long positions with prices 100, 102, and 104
  • A member has multiple short positions with prices 101 and 103
  • Member maintains a sufficient audit trail documenting the calculation
When
  • Member transfers a trade weighted average of longs and a trade weighted average of shorts
Then
  • The weighted average calculation is accepted
  • The audit trail demonstrates how the average price was calculated
  • The pricing basis used is documented
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-033-02: Members may transfer a single trade or a trade weighted average of the longs AND a trade weighted average of the shorts as long as they maintain a sufficient audit trail (p.11)

  • The action is rejected
  • Validation result equals reject

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-036-01-positive-01
Title: Execute reversal/correction using cancellation trade flag
Intent: Verify cancellation trade flag can be used to correct submission errors
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-036-01
Given
  • A trade was previously submitted with errors in one or more fields
  • The errors occurred at the point of trade submission
When
  • User enters trade data using the cancellation trade flag to execute a Reversal/Correction
Then
  • The correction is accepted
  • Trade data is entered with the correct values
  • Processing result is available
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-036-01: To execute a Reversal/Correction the cancellation trade flag should be used to correct errors that may have occurred in one or more of the fields entered into the Matching System at the point of trade submission (p.12)

Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-037-01
Given
  • A trade was previously submitted with Reversal/Correction trade category
  • The original trade has a registered trade category
When
  • User submits a new trade with the same Reversal/Correction trade category as the original
Then
  • The trade submission is accepted
  • The trade category matches the previously registered trade
  • Processing result is available
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-037-01: Trades previously submitted with a Reversal/Correction trade category must be submitted with the same trade category as the previously registered trade (p.12)

Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-037-01
Given
  • A trade was previously submitted with Reversal/Correction trade category
  • The original trade has a registered trade category
When

Then
  • The mismatch is detected
  • The obligation to match trade category is not fulfilled
  • Validation identifies the non-compliance

Findings
Evidence quote is truncated compared to positive test case. Uses only fragment: 'must be submitted with the same trade category as the previously registered trade' instead of full rule text 'Trades previously submitted with a Reversal/Correction trade category must be submitted with the same trade category as the previously registered trade'. While not a critical issue, evidence consistency could be improved by including the full rule context.

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-037-02

  • The original trade has a Matching Reference Number
  • A reversal/correction is needed

  • The Cancel Link Id is set to the Matching Reference Number of the original trade

  • The trade requires reversal
When
  • A trade is submitted with Reversal flag set

  • Only the Buy/Sell indicator differs from the original
Then
  • The trade is accepted by the Matching System

  • The trade requires Reversal/Correction
When

  • The trades are intended to counter the original registration
Then
  • The trades are accepted
  • The original registration is countered in the Matching System

Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B3-1

  • The trade requires Reversal/Correction
When

Then
  • An exception is raised
  • The system requires correction to set Cancellation flag to Reversal/Correction

Evidence

Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B3-3
Given
  • A clearing member has an existing registered Agreed Trade in the Matching System
  • The clearing member intends to counter the original registration
When

Then
  • The system accepts the two new trades
  • The original registration is countered by the new entries

SR-MR-060-A-1TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-positive-01UNA Mnemonic Use - Regulation 3.3 Compliancemissingnot_applicable
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-positive-01
Title: Member uses UNA mnemonic in Client Contracts with Regulation 3.3 compliance
Intent: Verify that UNA mnemonic use for Client Contracts registration is permitted when the member complies with Regulation 3.3 and Clearing House Rules
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-A-1
Given
  • A member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contract registration
  • The member is not facilitating Give-Up trades
When
  • The member enters trade data with UNA mnemonic
  • The member complies with Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations
  • The member allocates positions to the relevant position-keeping account at the Clearing House
Then
  • The system permits the use of the UNA mnemonic
  • The trade is successfully registered
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-060-A-1: Such use of the UNA mnemonic is permitted, provided that (a) Members comply with their obligations under Regulation 3.3 (p.13)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 2,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
CRITICAL: Semantic rule SR-MR-060-A-1 is classified as 'reference_only' with 'testability: non_testable' and 'coverage_eligible: false'
Evidence quote in test scenario (page 13) does not match the evidence quote in the semantic rule definition (page 18: 'Entered into an over-the-counter contract with another Member')
Cannot produce meaningful test cases against a rule explicitly marked as non-testable
Test design assumes testable workflow/outcome, but rule declares no executable assertions (dsl_assertions: [])

Title: Member uses UNA mnemonic without Regulation 3.3 compliance
Intent: Verify that UNA mnemonic use is rejected when the member fails to comply with Regulation 3.3 obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-A-1
Given
  • A member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contract registration
  • The member has not complied with Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations
When
  • The member enters trade data with UNA mnemonic
Then
  • The system rejects the UNA mnemonic use
  • The trade registration is not permitted
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-060-A-1: Such use of the UNA mnemonic is permitted, provided that (a) Members comply with their obligations under Regulation 3.3 (p.13)

SR-MR-060-A-1TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-exception-01UNA Mnemonic Use - Regulation 3.3 Compliancemissingnot_applicable
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-exception-01
Title: UNA mnemonic use inconsistent with broader LME Rulebook requirements
Intent: Verify that UNA mnemonic use is not permitted when it violates broader LME Rulebook requirements
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-A-1
Given
  • A member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contract registration
  • The member complies with Regulation 3.3
  • The use is inconsistent with broader requirements in the Matching Rules or LME Rulebook
When
  • The member enters trade data with UNA mnemonic
Then
  • The system rejects the UNA mnemonic use
  • An exception indicates violation of broader LME Rulebook requirements
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-060-A-1: such use is permissible under, and consistent with, the broader requirements set out in these Matching Rules and the LME Rulebook (p.13)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 2,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
CRITICAL: Same fundamental issue - rule is classified as non-testable reference_only
Evidence quote in scenario (page 13: 'such use is permissible under, and consistent with...') does not appear in the semantic rule definition's evidence list (which shows 'Entered into an over-the-counter contract with another Member' from page 18)
Exception test case is not applicable to a rule with no testable constraints or rejection pathways

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-exception-01
Title: UNA mnemonic use when member fails to ensure client compliance
Intent: Verify exception handling when member allocation prevents clients from complying with contractual obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-B-1
Given
  • A member uses UNA mnemonic for Client Contract registration
  • The position allocation does not enable the member and its Clients to comply with contractual obligations
When
  • The system detects that position allocation undermines compliance capability
Then
  • The system raises an exception
  • The UNA mnemonic use is flagged for review
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-060-B-1: enable the Member and its Client(s) to comply with any applicable legal or regulatory requirements and/or any contractual obligations (p.13)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}

Missing Aspects 
Findings

Scenario tests exception handling appropriately: when position allocation undermines member/client compliance capability, system should raise exception and flag UNA mnemonic for review.
Evidence alignment: 'enable the Member and its Client(s) to comply' is directly relevant to the scenario's compliance-undermining condition.
Case type 'exception' is appropriate for SR-MR-060-B-1 which contains a conditional obligation about position allocation enabling compliance.

SR-MR-060-C-1TC-SR-MR-060-C-1-exception-01UNA Mnemonic Use - LME Rulebook Consistencymissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-C-1-exception-01
Title: UNA mnemonic use after LME Rulebook amendment
Intent: Verify exception handling when UNA mnemonic use was consistent with previous LME Rulebook but is no longer consistent after amendment
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-C-1
Given
  • A member uses UNA mnemonic that was consistent with previous Matching Rules or LME Rulebook
  • The LME Rulebook has been amended since the original use
When
  • The system re-evaluates UNA mnemonic use against updated LME Rulebook
Then
  • The system flags an exception for re-evaluation
  • The use is marked for compliance review against amended rules
Assumptions
  • The rulebook amendments may occur after initial registration
Evidence
MR-060-C-1: in each case as amended from time to time (p.13)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Scenario introduces temporal versioning concept: UNA mnemonic use that was consistent with previous rules but becomes inconsistent after amendments.
Evidence quote 'in each case as amended from time to time' supports the versioning concept but does not explicitly define re-evaluation mechanics.
Weakness: The assumption 'rulebook amendments may occur after initial registration' implies a system behavior (re-evaluation) that is not explicitly defined in the semantic rule or evidence.
This scenario may be testing a hypothetical re-evaluation workflow that does not exist in the documented system behavior. Hallucination risk: medium.
Case type 'exception' is appropriate for testing amendment-driven re-evaluation if such workflow exists.

Title: Transaction with exclusive commodity transfer to single counterparty

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-A-1
Given
  • A transaction involves the transfer of commodities or guaranteed rights relating to title to commodities
  • An agreement governs the transfer
When
  • The seller transfers or pledges a particular commodity to only one counterparty at a time
Then
  • The system accepts the transaction
  • The financing arrangement is valid

Evidence

  • Processing result is recorded

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-046-01-negative-01
Title: Give-Up trade registration failure when omitted
Intent: Verify that omitting Give-Up trade registration results in failed obligation fulfillment
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-01
Given
  • A Give-Up trade has been concluded on an Execution Venue
When
  • The executing member fails to register the Give-Up trade in the Matching System
Then
  • The registration obligation is not fulfilled
  • A validation failure is recorded
Assumptions
  • The system detects missing registration
Evidence
MR-046-01: Give-Up trades must be registered in the Matching System (p.15)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct

Is Blocking: False

SR-MR-046-02TC-SR-MR-046-02-positive-01Give-Up Trade Entry Deadlinemissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-046-02-positive-01
Title: Give-Up Executor trade half entered within deadline
Intent: Verify that entering the Give-Up Executor trade half within 10 minutes of execution meets the deadline requirement
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-02
Given
  • A Client order has been executed
  • The executing member has the trade half data available
When
  • The executing member enters the Give-Up Executor trade half within 10 minutes of execution
Then
  • The deadline validation passes
  • The trade half is recorded with a valid timestamp
  • No late submission penalty is triggered
Assumptions
  • Submission occurs at 9 minutes after execution
Evidence
MR-046-02: Within 10 minutes from the time of Execution of the Client order, the executing Member must enter a Give-Up Executor trade half (p.15)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

SR-MR-046-02TC-SR-MR-046-02-boundary-01Give-Up Trade Entry Deadlinemissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-046-02-boundary-01
Title: Give-Up trade entry at exact deadline boundary
Intent: Verify system behavior when trade data entry occurs at the precise 10-minute deadline boundary
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-02
Given
  • A Client order has been executed at a known timestamp
  • The 10-minute deadline is precisely defined
When
  • The executing member enters the Give-Up Executor trade half at exactly 10 minutes from execution
Then
  • The system determines deadline compliance status
  • The recorded timestamp reflects the submission time
Assumptions
  • System clock precision is sufficient to distinguish exact boundary
Evidence
MR-046-02: Within 10 minutes from the time of Execution (p.15)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Ambiguity in boundary interpretation: The rule states 'Within 10 minutes' which typically means strictly less than 10 minutes. The scenario tests submission at 'exactly 10 minutes' without clarifying whether this should pass or fail.
Weak assertion: The 'then' clause states 'The system determines deadline compliance status' without specifying the expected outcome. This leaves the boundary pass/fail criterion undefined.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-046-02-negative-01
Title: Give-Up trade entry after deadline
Intent: Verify that entering trade data beyond the 10-minute deadline results in late submission status
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-02
Given
  • A Client order has been executed
  • The executing member delays trade data entry
When
  • The executing member enters the Give-Up Executor trade half after 10 minutes from execution
Then
  • The deadline validation fails
  • Late submission status is recorded
  • The submission is flagged as late
Assumptions
  • Submission occurs at 11 minutes after execution

  • The venue code is set to something other than Inter-office
When

Then
  • The validation result is reject
  • The trade submission is not permitted
  • An error or rejection message is returned

  • Venue code LME is used as the invalid example

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-046-04-negative-01
Title: Give-Up trade against prohibited venue code rejected
Intent: Verify that members cannot submit Give-Up trades against any venue code other than Inter-office
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-04
Given
  • A member attempts to submit a Give-Up trade
  • The venue code is any value other than Inter-office
When
  • The member submits the Give-Up trade to the Matching System
Then
  • The validation result is reject
  • The submission is not permitted
  • The prohibition is enforced
Assumptions
  • The prohibition applies to all non-Inter-office venue codes
Evidence
MR-046-04: Members are not permitted to submit Give-Up trades against any other venue code (p.15)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Incomplete rule coverage: Required case types for SR-MR-046-04 specify 'negative' and 'positive', but only the negative test case (TC-SR-MR-046-04-negative-01) is provided. The positive case verifying that Inter-office venue code is permitted is missing.
SR-MR-046-04TC-SR-MR-046-04-positive-01Give-Up Trade Venue Code Prohibitionmissinguncovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-046-04-positive-01
Title: Give-Up trade submission compliance with venue code prohibition
Intent: Verify that compliant Give-Up trade submissions with Inter-office venue code satisfy the prohibition requirement
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-04
Given
  • A member has a valid Give-Up trade to submit
  • The venue code is set to Inter-office
When
  • The member submits the Give-Up trade to the Matching System
Then
  • The validation result is accept
  • The trade is successfully submitted
  • No prohibition violation occurs
Assumptions
  • Inter-office is the sole permitted venue code
Evidence
MR-046-04: Members are not permitted to submit Give-Up trades against any other venue code (p.15)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: False
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 2,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
CRITICAL: case_type mismatch. Rule SR-MR-046-04 is a prohibition (must_not). This positive test does not validate the prohibition—it only verifies that compliant Inter-office submissions are accepted. A prohibition test must verify that violations (non-Inter-office venue codes) are rejected.
Coverage gap: The prohibition statement 'Members are not permitted to submit Give-Up trades against any other venue code' is not tested for enforcement. The test checks the happy path instead.
Evidence is weak: Quote 'Members are not permitted to submit Give-Up trades against any other venue code' is provided, but the test scenario does not exercise the prohibition constraint—only the permitted case.
Test design flaw: scenario 'then' clause expects 'accept' validation result, but prohibition rules should have 'reject' as the primary oracle for the negative path.

Case Type: positive

  • A member needs to comply with the 10-minute window for give-up submissions
When
  • The member elects to use the UNA process described in paragraphs 50 to 60
Then
  • The use of the UNA process is permitted as an optional facilitation mechanism
Assumptions
  • The member is not obligated to use the UNA process but may elect to do so

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-050-01-state_transition-01
Title: Trade state transitions from pending to matched upon give-up executor submission
Intent: Verify the automatic state transition that occurs when the matching system processes the give-up executor trade half
Priority: high
Case Type: state_transition
Paragraph IDs: MR-050-01
Given
  • A give-up executor trade half exists in pending state awaiting submission
When
  • The give-up executor trade half is submitted into the Matching System
Then
  • The matching system transitions the trade state and automatically generates the give-up clearer trade half with account field 'U'
Assumptions
  • The state transition is automatic upon valid submission
Evidence
MR-050-01: The submission of the Give-Up Executor trade half into the Matching System will automatically generate a Give-Up Clearer trade half, with an account field of 'U'. (p.16)
