SR-MR-056-A-1TC-SR-MR-056-A-1-exception-01Trade Details Amendment Workflow for Give-Upsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-056-A-1-exception-01
Title: Original trade details unchanged workflow path
Intent: Verify that when original trade details have not changed, the standard give-up workflow applies instead of the amended details path.
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-056-A-1
Given
  • Original trade details including Prompt Date, price, and volume remain unchanged
  • No amendments have been agreed with the client
When
  • The executing member chooses to follow either path (a) or (b) for give-up
  • Trade details are verified as unchanged
Then
  • The system applies the standard give-up workflow
  • The amended details workflow is not triggered
Assumptions
  • This rule only applies when details have changed; otherwise standard rules apply
Evidence
MR-056-A-1: Where the details of the original trade have changed – e.g. Prompt Date, price, volume – then the executing Member may again choose to follow either(a) or(b) as above. (p.17)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Exception case correctly identifies the precondition condition of the rule (details unchanged vs changed) and tests the alternate path
SR-MR-056-B-1Coverage: fully_covered | Req positivenegative|Present negativepositive|Accept negativepositive
SR-MR-056-B-1TC-SR-MR-056-B-1-positive-01Give-Up Executor Execution Time for Amended Trade Detailsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-056-B-1-positive-01
Title: Amended trade execution time set to client agreement time
Intent: Verify that when trade details are amended, the Execution Time must reflect when the amended details were agreed with the client.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-056-B-1
Given
  • Trade details have been amended with client agreement
  • The client agreed to amended details at time T_agreement
When
  • The Give-Up Executor trade half is submitted with Execution Time equal to T_agreement
Then
  • The system accepts the submission
  • The obligation is fulfilled correctly
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-056-B-1: the Execution Time of either the Give-Up Executor trade half must be the time at which the amended details of the trade were agreed with the Client. (p.17)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Positive case appropriately covers the obligation when Execution Time matches T_agreement. Evidence (MR-056-B-1, p.17) directly supports the test scenario. Only positive case tested; negative case TC-SR-MR-056-B-1-negative-01 also exists in batch, so full coverage achieved.
SR-MR-056-B-1TC-SR-MR-056-B-1-negative-01Give-Up Executor Execution Time for Amended Trade Detailsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-056-B-1-negative-01
Title: Amended trade execution time not set to client agreement time
Intent: Verify that when trade details are amended, failing to set the Execution Time to the client agreement time results in rejection.
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-056-B-1
Given
  • Trade details have been amended with client agreement at time T_agreement
  • The submitted Execution Time is T_incorrect
When
  • The Give-Up Executor trade half is submitted where T_incorrect does not equal T_agreement
Then
  • The system rejects the submission
  • The obligation is not fulfilled
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-056-B-1: the Execution Time of either the Give-Up Executor trade half must be the time at which the amended details of the trade were agreed with the Client. (p.17)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Negative case appropriately tests violation of the obligation (Execution Time ≠ T_agreement). Evidence (MR-056-B-1, p.17) supports rejection outcome. Good pairing with positive case.

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-058-01-positive-01
Title: Give-up not possible on T triggers cleared trade processing
Intent: Verify that when a give-up cannot be effected on trade date T, the cleared trade is processed by the Clearing House.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-058-01
Given
  • A Give-Up Executor trade half has been submitted
  • A system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half exists
  • A Cleared Trade has been created from both trade halves
  • It was not possible to effect the give-up on trade date T
When
  • The Cleared Trade is submitted for processing
Then
  • The Clearing House processes the Cleared Trade
  • The Cleared Trade is processed together with all other Cleared Trades in accordance with Clearing House Rules
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-058-01: the Cleared Trade, created through the submission of the Give-Up Executor trade half and the system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half, will be processed by the Clearing House together with all other Cleared Trades in ... (p.17)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}

Missing Aspects

Positive case correctly tests Cleared Trade processing when give-up not effected on T. Evidence (MR-058-01, p.17) directly supports the scenario. State transition case TC-SR-MR-058-01-state_transition-01 also exists in batch.

Title: State transition from give-up pending to cleared trade processed
Intent: Verify the state transition that occurs when a give-up not processed on T transitions to cleared trade status.
Priority: high
Case Type: state_transition
Paragraph IDs: MR-058-01
Given
  • Give-Up Executor trade half is submitted
  • System-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half is created
  • Give-up cannot be effected on trade date T
When
  • The workflow event triggers processing
  • The Cleared Trade state changes
Then
  • The trade state transitions to processed status
  • The resulting trade state reflects cleared status
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-058-01: the Cleared Trade, created through the submission of the Give-Up Executor trade half and the system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half, will be processed by the Clearing House together with all other Cleared Trades in ... (p.17)

SR-MR-059-01TC-SR-MR-059-01-positive-01Give-Up Processing Deadline on T+1missingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-059-01-positive-01
Title: Give-up processed before 10:00 London time on T+1
Intent: Verify that give-ups not processed on T are successfully processed when submitted before the T+1 deadline.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-01
Given
  • A give-up was not processed on trade date T
  • The current time is before 10:00 London time on T+1
When
  • The Give-Up is submitted for processing on T+1
Then
  • The deadline validation result is pass
  • The Give-Up is processed successfully
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-059-01: The deadline for processing Give-Ups that have not been processed on T, is 10:00(London time) on T+1. (p.18)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores

}

Missing Aspects

Positive deadline case correctly tests submission before 10:00 London time on T+1. Evidence (MR-059-01, p.18) directly supports. Boundary and negative cases also exist in batch.
SR-MR-059-01TC-SR-MR-059-01-boundary-01Give-Up Processing Deadline on T+1missingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-059-01-boundary-01
Title: Give-up processed exactly at 10:00 London time on T+1
Intent: Verify the boundary condition when a give-up is submitted exactly at the deadline time.
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-01
Given
  • A give-up was not processed on trade date T
  • The current time is exactly 10:00:00 London time on T+1
When
  • The Give-Up is submitted for processing at exactly 10:00 London time on T+1
Then
  • The deadline validation result is pass
  • The submission is accepted as within deadline
Assumptions
  • Exact interpretation of deadline inclusion: at exactly 10:00 is considered on-time
Evidence
MR-059-01: The deadline for processing Give-Ups that have not been processed on T, is 10:00(London time) on T+1. (p.18)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores

  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Boundary case correctly tests submission at exactly 10:00:00 London time. Assumption about deadline inclusion is reasonable. Full set of deadline case types (positive, boundary, negative) achieved.

Title: Give-up processed after 10:00 London time on T+1

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-01
Given
  • A give-up was not processed on trade date T
  • The current time is after 10:00 London time on T+1
When
  • The Give-Up is submitted for processing after 10:00 London time on T+1
Then
  • The deadline validation result indicates late submission
  • The system records the submission as late
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-059-01: The deadline for processing Give-Ups that have not been processed on T, is 10:00(London time) on T+1. (p.18)

SR-MR-059-03TC-SR-MR-059-03-positive-01Exchange Information Request for Late Give-Upsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-059-03-positive-01
Title: Member may be required to provide additional information
Intent: Verify that members understand they may be requested to provide additional information to explain why the give-up was not processed on T.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-03
Given
  • A give-up was not processed on trade date T
  • A Give-Up Executor trade half has been submitted
When
  • The Exchange requests additional information regarding the late processing
Then
  • The member may be required to provide such additional information
  • The information should demonstrate why the Give-Up was not possible on T
Assumptions
  • This is a permission/obligation on the Exchange to request, not a requirement on the member to always provide
Evidence
MR-059-03: Members should be aware that they may be required to provide the Exchange with such additional information as the Exchange may request from time to time to demonstrate why it was not possible to process the Give-Up on T. (p.18)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
The rule (permission: 'may be required') describes a potential Exchange request for information. The scenario appropriately tests member awareness. Minor semantic nuance: the rule is a permission/obligation on the Exchange to request, not a strict requirement on the member. The scenario captures this through the assumption. Evidence (MR-059-03, p.18) supports the scenario.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-061-01-positive-01
Title: OTC Bring-On Trade Treated as Standard Agreed Trade
Intent: Verify that an Agreed Trade submitted with OTC Bring-On category is treated the same as any other Agreed Trade from the inter-office market
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-061-01
Given
  • A Member has an Agreed Trade with the OTC Bring-On category
  • The trade details reflect the terms of an over-the-counter contract
When
  • The trade is submitted to the Matching System with OTC Bring-On category
Then
  • The system accepts the trade
  • The trade is processed in the same manner as any other Agreed Trade from the inter-office market
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-061-01: An Agreed Trade that is submitted with the OTC Bring-On category shall therefore be treated in the same way as any other Agreed Trade made in the inter-office market. (p.18)

SR-MR-062-02TC-SR-MR-062-02-positive-01Exchange and Clearing House Liability Limitationmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-062-02-positive-01
Title: No Exchange or Clearing House Liability for OTC Contracts
Intent: Verify that neither the Exchange nor the Clearing House assumes responsibility or liability for original OTC contracts
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-062-02
Given
  • An original over-the-counter contract exists
  • The contract is associated with an Agreed Trade via Contingent Agreement to Trade
When
  • The Agreed Trade is Executed
  • Parties seek recourse related to the original OTC contract
Then
  • The Exchange does not accept liability for the original OTC contract
  • The Clearing House does not accept liability for the original OTC contract
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-062-02: Neither the Exchange nor the Clearing House shall have any responsibility or liability to any person in respect of the original over-the-counter contract. (p.18)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

}

Title: Liability Rejection for OTC Contract Matters

Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-062-02
Given
  • A dispute arises regarding the original over-the-counter contract
  • The Exchange or Clearing House is named in the dispute
When
  • Parties attempt to hold Exchange or Clearing House liable for the original OTC contract terms
Then
  • The system rejects liability claims
  • The Exchange and Clearing House disclaim responsibility
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-062-02: Neither the Exchange nor the Clearing House shall have any responsibility or liability to any person in respect of the original over-the-counter contract. (p.18)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-063-04-positive-01
Title: OTC Backloading Service Trades Treated as Standard Agreed Trades
Intent: Verify that any Agreed Trade submitted via OTC Backloading Service is treated the same as standard Contingent Agreement to Trade trades from inter-office market
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-063-04
Given
  • A Member submits an Agreed Trade to the Matching System via OTC Backloading Service
  • The trade originates from the OTC Backloading Service process
When
  • The trade is processed in the Matching System
Then
  • The Exchange treats the trade in the same manner as any other Agreed Trade from inter-office market
  • Standard Contingent Agreement to Trade rules apply
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-063-04: The Exchange shall treat any Agreed Trade submitted to the Matching System resulting from an OTC Backloading Service in the same manner as any other Agreed Trade represented by a Contingent Agreement to Trade agreed in t... (p.18)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-063-04-negative-01
Title: Non-Backloading Service Trade Not Treated as OTC Backloading Trade
Intent: Verify that trades not originating from OTC Backloading Service are not treated under OTC Backloading Service rules
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-063-04
Given
  • A Member submits a standard Agreed Trade not from OTC Backloading Service
When
  • The trade is processed in the Matching System
  • Party attempts to apply OTC Backloading Service treatment
Then
  • The system does not apply OTC Backloading Service specific rules
  • The trade is processed under its actual category
Assumptions 
Evidence

SR-MR-064-A-1Coverage: uncovered | Req positivenegative|Present negativepositive|Missing negativepositive
SR-MR-064-A-1TC-SR-MR-064-A-1-positive-01OTC Transaction Mandatory Requirementsmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-A-1-positive-01
Title: OTC Transaction Meets All Mandatory Requirements
Intent: Verify that an OTC transaction that meets all mandatory requirements (i, ii, iii) is accepted for processing
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-A-1
Given
  • An OTC transaction exists with complete details
  • All mandatory requirements for the transaction are being evaluated
When
  • The transaction is submitted and all mandatory conditions are satisfied
Then
  • The transaction meets the mandatory requirements
  • The system proceeds with processing
Assumptions
  • The specific requirements i, ii, iii are defined in the OTC transaction specification
Evidence
MR-064-A-1: (a) the over-the-counter transaction must: i. ii. iii. (p.19)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence for MR-064-A-1 is extremely weak: quote '(a) the over-the-counter transaction must: i. ii. iii.' does not specify what requirements i, ii, and iii actually are
Assumption stated: 'The specific requirements i, ii, iii are defined in the OTC transaction specification' - these specific requirements are not provided in the evidence
Test design relies on assumption rather than concrete rule details
Cannot verify if positive case truly covers all mandatory requirements without seeing the specific i, ii, iii content
SR-MR-064-A-1TC-SR-MR-064-A-1-negative-01OTC Transaction Mandatory Requirementsmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-A-1-negative-01
Title: OTC Transaction Fails Mandatory Requirements
Intent: Verify that an OTC transaction failing any mandatory requirement is rejected or flagged
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-A-1
Given
  • An OTC transaction exists with incomplete or non-compliant details
  • One or more mandatory requirements are not satisfied
When
  • The transaction is submitted for validation
  • The system checks mandatory conditions i, ii, and iii
Then
  • The transaction fails validation
  • The system rejects or flags the transaction for non-compliance
Assumptions
  • The specific requirements i, ii, iii are defined in the OTC transaction specification
Evidence
MR-064-A-1: (a) the over-the-counter transaction must: i. ii. iii. (p.19)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence for MR-064-A-1 is extremely weak: quote '(a) the over-the-counter transaction must: i. ii. iii.' does not specify what requirements i, ii, and iii actually are
Negative case is logically derived but cannot verify specificity without knowing what i, ii, iii are
Test assumes incomplete/non-compliant details will fail but cannot specify which exact requirements would be violated

SR-MR-064-B-1TC-SR-MR-064-B-1-positive-01Contingent Agreement to Trade Booking Requirementmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-B-1-positive-01
Title: Both counterparties successfully book the transaction
Intent: Verify that when both counterparties independently book the same transaction details, the obligation is fulfilled
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-B-1
Given
  • A Contingent Agreement to Trade exists between Member A and Member B
  • The agreement specifies terms of an over-the-counter contract
When
  • Member A submits transaction details reflecting the OTC contract terms to their trading system
  • Member B submits identical transaction details reflecting the OTC contract terms to their trading system
Then
  • The transaction is booked by both counterparties
  • The matching system confirms both bookings
Assumptions
  • Both submissions occur within the same business day
  • System connectivity between both counterparties and LME is operational
Evidence
MR-064-B-1: have been booked by both counterparties to the transaction (p.19)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

Title: Only one counterparty books the transaction
Intent: Verify that the obligation fails when both counterparties do not book the transaction
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-B-1
Given
  • A Contingent Agreement to Trade exists between Member A and Member B
When
  • Only Member A submits transaction details to their trading system
  • Member B fails to submit the corresponding transaction details
Then
  • The transaction is not fully booked
  • The matching system flags a missing booking from Member B
  • The obligation is not fulfilled
Assumptions

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-B-2-positive-01
Title: Transaction booked within business day cutoff
Intent: Verify that booking within the business day cutoff passes deadline validation
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-B-2
Given
  • A Contingent Agreement to Trade has been established
  • The current time is within business hours on a business day
When
  • The transaction details are submitted and booked within the trading system
Then
  • The deadline validation result is pass
  • The recorded timestamp shows booking within the business day cutoff
Assumptions
  • Trading system clock is synchronized to official time
Evidence
MR-064-B-2: be booked within their trading systems (p.19)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-B-2-boundary-01
Title: Transaction booked exactly at business day cutoff
Intent: Verify boundary behavior when booking occurs at the exact deadline moment
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-B-2
Given
  • A Contingent Agreement to Trade has been established
  • The current time is exactly at the business day cutoff
When
  • The transaction details are submitted and booked at the exact cutoff time
Then
  • The system accepts the booking as within cutoff
  • The deadline validation result is pass
  • The recorded timestamp reflects the submission time
Assumptions
  • System processes timestamp with sufficient precision to distinguish cutoff moments
Evidence
MR-064-B-2: be booked within their trading systems (p.19)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores

  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote 'be booked within their trading systems' does not explicitly define behavior at the exact cutoff moment; boundary interpretation is inferred rather than specified in source

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-B-2-negative-01
Title: Transaction booking exceeds business day cutoff
Intent: Verify that booking after the business day cutoff fails deadline validation
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-B-2
Given
  • A Contingent Agreement to Trade has been established
  • The current time is after the business day cutoff
When
  • The transaction details are submitted after the cutoff time
Then
  • The deadline validation result is late_submission
  • The system rejects or flags the late submission
  • The booking is not recorded as compliant

SR-MR-064-B-4TC-SR-MR-064-B-4-positive-01OTC Bring-On Trade Date Sequencing Requirementmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-064-B-4-positive-01
Title: Original OTC trade date precedes OTC Bring-On trade date
Intent: Verify that the obligation is fulfilled when the original OTC transaction has an earlier trade date
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-064-B-4
Given
  • An original over-the-counter transaction exists
  • The original OTC transaction has a trade date of T_minus
When
  • An OTC Bring-On trade is submitted with a trade date of T_plus where T_minus < T_plus
Then
  • The processing result is not null
  • The OTC Bring-On is accepted
  • The trade date sequence validation passes
Assumptions
  • Both trades are submitted with valid venue codes
Evidence
MR-064-B-4: and the trade date of the original over-the-counter transaction must be prior to the trade date of the OTC Bring-On. (p.19)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

  • The original OTC transaction has a trade date of T
When

Then
  • The processing result is null or error
  • The OTC Bring-On is rejected
  • The trade date sequence validation fails

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-065-01-positive-01
Title: OTC Take Off category successfully converts Client Contract to OTC contract
Intent: Verify that the OTC Take Off category permission allows conversion of a Client Contract to an OTC contract
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-065-01
Given
  • A Client Contract exists between a member and their client
When
  • The member initiates an OTC Take Off using the OTC Take Off trade category
  • The conversion process is executed
Then
  • The processing result is not null
  • The Client Contract is converted to an OTC contract
  • The action is allowed
Assumptions
  • All other prerequisites for conversion are met
Evidence
MR-065-01: The OTC Take Off category may be used to convert a Client Contract to an OTC contract. (p.19)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects

  • The member provides full details of the relevant Client Contract

  • The Client Contract details are recorded
  • The obligation is fulfilled

Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-066-02
Given
  • An original Client Contract exists with specific contractual terms
When
  • An OTC Take Off is created with identical contractual commitment to the original Client Contract
Then
  • The processing result is not null
  • The OTC Take Off is validated as matching
  • The obligation is fulfilled
Assumptions
  • All contractual terms (quantity, price, maturity, etc.) are identical
Evidence
MR-066-02: The OTC Take Off created must be for the same contractual commitment as the original Client Contract. (p.19)

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-067-01
Given
  • An OTC Take Off transaction has been executed
When
  • The member maintains a full audit trail for the OTC Take Off transaction
  • The audit trail includes the client instructions to take each trade off exchange

  • The processing result is not null
  • The audit trail is recorded
  • The obligation is fulfilled
Assumptions
  • Audit trail includes timestamp, user ID, and transaction details

Case Type: positive

  • An OTC contract is submitted for processing
When
  • The Exchange processes the OTC contract
Then
  • The Exchange accepts the OTC contract
  • No liability is assumed by the Exchange
  • No liability is assumed by the Clearing House

Title: FPA starts automatically upon PTT Initiating Pair receipt

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-069-01
Given
  • An active Matching System is operational
  • Trading Regulation 2.15 is applicable
When
  • An IOM PTT Initiating Pair is received into the Matching System
Then
  • An FPA automatically starts
  • FPA details are disseminated via LME Market Data feeds
  • Fixed parameters including contract information are disseminated

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-069-01-state_transition-01
Title: State transition from idle to FPA active
Intent: Verify the system transitions from idle state to FPA active state when triggering event occurs
Priority: high
Case Type: state_transition
Paragraph IDs: MR-069-01
Given
  • The Matching System is in idle state with no active FPA
  • An IOM PTT Initiating Pair is ready for submission
When
  • The IOM PTT Initiating Pair is received into the Matching System
Then
  • System state changes from idle to FPA active
  • Auction ID is generated
  • FPA details are disseminated to market data feeds
  • Market participants can now submit bids and offers
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-069-01: an FPA will automatically start with the FPA details and fixed parameters(contract information) disseminated via the LME Market Data feeds (p.19)

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-069-03
Given
  • The current time is after 12:30
  • A TOM IOM PTT Initiating Pair is being submitted
When
  • Either leg of the IOM PTT Initiating Pair for TOM is received with 'Normal' trade category
Then
  • The Matching System automatically rejects the submission
  • The rejection is processed without manual intervention
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-069-03: If either leg of an IOM PTT Initiating Pair for TOM has been received after 12:30 using the 'Normal' trade category the Matching System will automatically reject the submission. (p.19)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-069-03-state_transition-01
Title: State transition to rejected state for late TOM Normal submission
Intent: Verify the system transitions to rejected state when late TOM submission with Normal category is detected
Priority: high
Case Type: state_transition
Paragraph IDs: MR-069-03
Given
  • The Matching System is in ready state to receive PTT submissions
  • A TOM IOM PTT Initiating Pair with 'Normal' trade category is being processed
When
  • The submission timestamp is confirmed to be after 12:30
Then
  • System state transitions to rejected
  • Submission is not accepted into the matching queue
  • Automatic rejection is logged
  • Relevant parties receive rejection notification
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-069-03: the Matching System will automatically reject the submission (p.19)

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-070-01
Given
  • An active FPA exists in the Matching System
  • The current time is within the Auction Window
When
  • A member submits a bid or offer into the matching system for the active FPA
Then
  • The bid or offer is accepted into the matching system
  • The submission timestamp is recorded

Evidence
MR-070-01: Members wishing to participate in an active FPA can submit bids and offers into the matching system within the Auction Window. (p.20)

SR-MR-070-01TC-SR-MR-070-01-boundary-01FPA Auction Window Submission Periodmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-070-01-boundary-01
Title: Submission at Auction Window boundary
Intent: Verify system handles bid/offer submission exactly at Auction Window boundary correctly
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-070-01
Given
  • An active FPA exists in the Matching System
  • The Auction Window has defined start and end times
When
  • A member submits a bid or offer at the exact moment the Auction Window opens or closes
Then
  • Submission at opening boundary is accepted if within window
  • Submission at closing boundary is accepted if timestamp is at or before close
  • Appropriate timestamp precision is applied
Assumptions
  • Precise timestamp resolution is system-defined
  • Timezone for auction window is consistent with system time
Evidence
MR-070-01: within the Auction Window (p.20)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote 'within the Auction Window' is valid but provides limited specificity for exact boundary timing expectations; scenario assumptions about timestamp precision are maker-inferred and not explicitly stated in source rule

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-070-02-positive-01
Title: Valid FPA submission with Auction ID and fixed parameters
Intent: Verify bids and offers submitted with correct Auction ID and fixed parameters are accepted
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-070-02
Given
  • An active FPA exists with a known Auction ID
  • The Auction trade has defined fixed parameters including price and shape
When
  • A member submits a bid or offer with the active FPA Auction ID in the Public Reference field
  • The submission includes the same fixed parameters as the auction trade
Then
  • The submission is validated successfully
  • The bid or offer is accepted into the matching system
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-070-02: Bids and offers will be required to include the active FPA Auction ID in the Public Reference field (p.20)
MR-070-02: entering the fixed parameters(e.g. same price and same shape for a carry as the auction trade) within the relevant LMEsmart fields (p.20)

  • An active FPA exists with defined fixed parameters
When

  • Submission is rejected with parameter validation error
  • Member is notified of missing fields

Then
  • The volume is accepted and recorded
  • The bid/offer is processed with the entered volume
  • The submission is valid regardless of volume difference

Title: Valid auction bid submission with correct parameters
Intent: Verify that auction bids/offers with correct counterparty, category, price type, and venue are accepted
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-070-04
Given
  • A counterparty is preparing to submit an auction bid/offer
When
  • Submitting auction bids/offers with counterparty "AUC", Category "Normal", Price Type "Current", and Venue "Inter-Office"
Then
  • The submission is accepted by the system
  • The trade is processed with the required parameters
Assumptions
  • Auction session is active and valid
  • All required trade fields are present

MR-070-04: When submitting auction bids/offers, the counterparty must be "AUC" (p.20)
MR-070-04: Category, Price Type and Venue must be "Normal", "Current" and "Inter-Office" respectively (p.20)

SR-MR-071-A-1Coverage: fully_covered | Req positivenegativeexception|Present exceptionnegativepositive|Accept exceptionnegativepositive

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-071-A-1-positive-01
Title: Valid auction bid submission with valid Auction ID
Intent: Verify that bids/offers with valid Auction ID are not rejected for this condition
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-071-A-1
Given
  • A counterparty is submitting an auction bid/offer
When
  • Submitting a bid/offer with a valid, non-expired Auction ID
Then
  • The bid/offer is not rejected due to Auction ID validation
  • Processing continues to other validations
Assumptions
  • Auction session is active
  • Other parameters meet requirements
Evidence
MR-071-A-1: Any bids or offers containing the following will be rejected on entry: (a) an invalid or expired Auction ID (p.20)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Positive test case verifies the happy path where valid Auction ID avoids rejection - this is appropriate for workflow validation
The 'then' clause ('processing continues to other validations') is inferred rather than directly stated in the rule; the rule only specifies what causes rejection, not what happens when it does not

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-071-C-1-exception-01
Title: Auction bid rejection with multiple incorrect parameter values
Intent: Verify system behavior when multiple parameters in the combination are incorrect
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-071-C-1
Given
  • A counterparty is submitting an auction bid/offer
When
  • Submitting a bid/offer with Category "Abnormal", Price Type "Future", and Venue "Floor"
Then
  • The bid/offer is rejected on entry
  • System handles multiple validation failures appropriately
Assumptions
  • All three required parameters deviate from the required combination
Evidence
MR-071-C-1: a combination of Category, Price Type and Venue other than "Normal", "Current" and "Inter-Office" (p.20)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores

  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}

Missing Aspects

Strong evidence with complete quote
Well-structured exception test case with multiple parameters deviating (Category='Abnormal', Price Type='Future', Venue='Floor')
Appropriate use of exception case type for edge case validation

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-072-D-1-positive-01
Title: Bids and offers matched with differing volumes (partial fills)
Intent: Verify that bids and offers may be matched based on differing volumes for partial fills
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-072-D-1
Given
  • Bids and offers are present in the Matching System at auction end
When
  • Matching bids and offers of differing volumes where partial fill criteria are met
Then
  • The system may match the bids and offers based on differing volumes
  • Partial fills are created successfully
Assumptions
  • Partial fills meet the criteria and limitations specified in Trading Regulation 2.15.8

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-073-01
Given
  • An auction is in progress with bids and offers present
When
  • The auction reaches its end time
Then
  • The Matching System automatically matches up successful bids and offers
  • Matched trades are created for submitting to clearing
Assumptions
  • Bids and offers meet all validity criteria
  • Auction timing mechanisms are functioning correctly

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-073-01-state_transition-01

Case Type: state_transition
Paragraph IDs: MR-073-01

  • Successful bids and offers have been submitted to the Matching System
When
  • The auction end event is triggered
Then
  • The state transition occurs automatically
  • Trades transition from submitted state to matched state
  • The resulting trade state is "matched"
Assumptions
  • State transition is automatic per the rule specification

Title: OTC Bring-On trade processed as Out of Scope Order
Intent: Verify that OTC Bring-On trades are correctly identified and processed as Out of Scope Orders
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-A-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker is processing OTC Bring-On trades
When
  • Submitting an OTC Bring-On trade for processing
Then
  • The trade is correctly categorized as an Out of Scope Order
  • Processing follows Out of Scope Order workflow as per Regulation 2.15
Assumptions
  • Trade meets OTC Bring-On criteria per paragraphs 14 and 60-68

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-A-1-negative-01
Title: Non-OTC trade incorrectly marked as OTC Bring-On rejected
Intent: Verify that trades that do not meet OTC Bring-On criteria are not processed as such
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-A-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker is processing a trade
When
  • Attempting to process a non-OTC trade as OTC Bring-On
Then
  • The trade is not accepted as OTC Bring-On
  • Appropriate error or alternative categorization is returned
Assumptions
  • The trade does not meet OTC Bring-On criteria per paragraphs 14 and 60-68
Evidence
MR-074-A-1: (a) OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off(as described in paragraphs 14 and 60 – 68) (p.21)

Negative scenario is valid but relies on inference that non-qualifying trades are rejected; the rule itself does not explicitly state rejection behavior for non-OTC trades marked as OTC Bring-On.

Title: OTC Bring-On trade with missing required references
Intent: Verify system behavior when OTC Bring-On trade has incomplete information
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-A-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker is processing an OTC Bring-On trade
When
  • Submitting an OTC Bring-On trade with missing required paragraph references or documentation
Then
  • The trade is handled according to exception procedures
  • System processes the exception appropriately
Assumptions
  • Trade has OTC characteristics but missing required supporting information

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-positive-01
Title: OTC Bring-On trade classified as Out of Scope Order
Intent: Verify that an OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off trade is correctly classified as an Out of Scope Order under Regulation 2.15
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-C-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker has an OTC Bring-On trade
  • The trade has a valid venue code
When
  • The broker corrects the OTC Bring-On trade and requests Out of Scope classification
Then
  • The trade is classified as an Out of Scope Order
  • The workflow progresses to permitted_branch_completed
Assumptions
  • The OTC Bring-On trade meets all criteria described in paragraphs 14 and 60-68
  • The trade is subject to Regulation 2.15 of the Trading Regulations

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-negative-01
Title: Non-OTC Bring-On trade rejected for Out of Scope classification
Intent: Verify that a trade that does not qualify as OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off is not classified as an Out of Scope Order
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-C-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker has a trade that is not an OTC Bring-On trade
  • The trade does not belong to any Out of Scope category
When
  • The broker attempts to correct the trade and request Out of Scope classification
Then
  • The trade is not classified as an Out of Scope Order
  • The workflow does not progress via the permitted branch
Assumptions
  • The trade category is not OTC Bring-On, OTC Take-Off, Transfer, Reversal/Correction, Financing, or Exception
  • The trade is subject to Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
Evidence
MR-074-C-1: This section details the trade categories, orders for which, are to be treated as "Out of Scope Orders" (p.21)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is weak: quote 'This section details the trade categories, orders for which, are to be treated as "Out of Scope Orders"' is generic and does not specifically support the negative scenario for OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off exclusion
Semantic rule SR-MR-074-C-1 is missing TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-positive-01 from the batch, resulting in only partial coverage
The semantic_rule statement.object incorrectly uses 'otc_bring_on_trade' but this appears to be a template error from first-pass generation; the scenario correctly uses the appropriate trade type in its given/when/then clauses
SR-MR-074-C-1TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-exception-01Out of Scope Order Classification for OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Offmissingpartial
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-exception-01
Title: OTC Bring-On trade with invalid venue code
Intent: Verify handling when an otherwise eligible OTC Bring-On trade fails due to invalid venue code
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-C-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker has an OTC Bring-On trade
  • The trade belongs to a category eligible for Out of Scope classification
When
  • The broker corrects the OTC Bring-On trade but the venue code is invalid or unrecognised
Then
  • The Out of Scope classification is not applied
  • A validation result is returned indicating the processing failure
Assumptions
  • The venue code validation occurs as a prerequisite to Out of Scope classification
  • The processing_result field will be populated with error details rather than null
Evidence
MR-074-C-1: OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off (as described in paragraphs 14 and 60 – 68) (p.21)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is weak: quote 'OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off (as described in paragraphs 14 and 60 – 68)' does not establish venue validation as a prerequisite condition; this appears to be an inferred requirement not directly stated in the rule
The venue code validation prerequisite is an assumption not substantiated by the semantic rule evidence
Positive case TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-positive-01 is missing from batch, reducing overall rule coverage

Intent: Verify handling when an otherwise eligible Financing trade fails due to invalid venue code
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-E-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker has a Financing trade
  • The trade belongs to a category eligible for Out of Scope classification
When
  • The broker corrects the Financing trade but the venue code is invalid or unrecognised
Then
  • The Out of Scope classification is not applied
  • A validation result is returned indicating the processing failure
Assumptions
  • The venue code validation occurs as a prerequisite to Out of Scope classification
  • The processing_result field will be populated with error details rather than null
Evidence
MR-074-E-1: Financing (as described in paragraphs 14 and 44 – 45) (p.21)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is weak: quote about Financing description does not establish venue code validation as a prerequisite
Venue validation prerequisite appears to be an inferred assumption rather than documented in rule evidence
Exception case_type is appropriate for testing invalid venue handling
SR-MR-075-01Coverage: uncovered | Req negativepositive|Missing negativepositive
SR-MR-075-01TC-SR-MR-075-01-negative-01Prohibition on Misuse of OTC Bring-On Trade Categorymissingmissing
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-075-01-negative-01
Title: OTC Bring-On trade category misused to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
Intent: Verify that a Member is prohibited from misusing the OTC Bring-On trade category to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements and Regulation 2.15
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-075-01
Given
  • A Member has an Inter-Office Order
  • The Inter-Office Order is subject to Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
When
  • The Member uses the OTC Bring-On trade category on the Inter-Office Order to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements and Regulation 2.15
Then
  • The use of the OTC Bring-On trade category is rejected
  • The validation_result is set to reject
  • The action is not permitted
Assumptions
  • The system can detect when OTC Bring-On is being used on an Inter-Office Order
  • The system enforces Regulation 2.15 compliance
Evidence
MR-075-01: A Member shall not mis-use the OTC Bring-On trade category (p.21)
Case Type:
Case Type Accepted:
Coverage Relevance:
Blocking Findings Count:
Is Blocking:
Scores
{}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings 
SR-MR-075-01TC-SR-MR-075-01-positive-01Prohibition on Misuse of OTC Bring-On Trade Categorymissingmissing
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-075-01-positive-01
Title: OTC Bring-On trade category correctly applied to OTC trade
Intent: Verify that the OTC Bring-On trade category is permitted when used correctly on an OTC trade
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-075-01
Given
  • A Member has a legitimate OTC Bring-On trade
  • The trade is an actual over-the-counter transaction
When
  • The Member uses the OTC Bring-On trade category on the OTC trade
Then
  • The use of the OTC Bring-On trade category is accepted
  • The trade is classified as an Out of Scope Order under Regulation 2.15
  • Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements do not apply
Assumptions
  • The OTC Bring-On trade meets the criteria described in paragraphs 14 and 60-68
  • The trade is genuinely an over-the-counter transaction and not an Inter-Office Order
Evidence
MR-075-01: A Member shall not mis-use the OTC Bring-On trade category (p.21)

Case Type Accepted:
Coverage Relevance:
Blocking Findings Count:
Is Blocking:
